region_of_origin: USA (McDonald's global product, introduced 1983; Singapore market variant)
food_subgroup: Deep-fried chicken nuggets with wheat batter coating; fast food / QSR product

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for Chicken McNuggets; FOODON_03315674 (fried chicken product) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GBY is the closest FoodEx2 category for fast food chicken nuggets; no exact code for McDonald's branded product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for this specific fast food product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of McDonald's Chicken McNuggets in Singapore is 6 pieces weighing approximately 98 grams, providing 258 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken McNuggets 6pc — McDonald's Singapore Official Nutritional Data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition page for the 6-piece Chicken McNuggets (98g serving). Per-100g values derived by scaling from the 98g serving data. McDonald's Singapore is treated as a verified brand-owner primary source for its own product nutritional data, consistent with USDA Branded Food category precedent for commercial food products. Micronutrient values (vitamins, minerals) cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central entry for chicken nuggets (USDA SR Legacy / Branded Foods) to confirm plausibility.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried battered chicken products — analogous estimate based on wheat-flour battered fried chicken; GI ~55 consistent with low-GI fried battered foods in the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~55 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour battered fried chicken products with comparable carbohydrate composition. The wheat flour batter coating is the primary carbohydrate source (~16g per 100g). Fat content (~14g per 100g) moderates glucose absorption. According to the University of Sydney GI Database, a GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. GL per 6-piece serving (98g) = (55 × 16.3 × 0.98) ÷ 100 ≈ 8.8. This estimate is consistent with published GI values for similar fried battered chicken products in peer-reviewed literature.

hypertension.guidance: A 6-piece serving contributes approximately 430mg sodium (~19% of the 2,300mg daily limit); sodium escalates significantly with larger portions (20-piece: ~1,430mg) or high-sodium dipping sauces (+190–260mg each), making portion control and sauce avoidance critical for hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~55, GL ~8.8 per 6-piece serving; portion escalation risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 6-piece serving has a manageable glycaemic load of ~8.8, and the 14g fat content moderates postprandial glucose rise. With a low GI of 55, a single serving is generally suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, but portion escalation to 20-piece servings raises GL to approximately 29.7, which is clinically significant for glycaemic management.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying in palm oil; saturated fat 2g per 100g; sodium 439mg per 100g; ultra-processed product
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Deep-frying at high temperatures generates pro-inflammatory oxidation products (aldehydes, ALEs) from palm oil; combined with moderate saturated fat, elevated sodium, and ultra-processed formulation, regular consumption is not recommended for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: McDonald's Chicken McNuggets (Singapore market) are bite-sized deep-fried chicken pieces coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter, sold in a 6-piece serving of approximately 98 grams. Each 6-piece serving provides approximately 258 kcal, 14g protein, 14g fat, and 16g carbohydrate, with 430mg sodium. The nuggets are made from chicken breast meat formed into four shapes (bell, ball, boot, and bow-tie), coated in a light wheat batter, and deep-fried in palm oil. McDonald's Singapore confirmed removal of artificial preservatives from the nugget coating in 2024. The product is certified halal and is dairy-free. Texture is crispy on the outside with a tender, moist chicken interior. GI is estimated at ~55 (low), with a glycaemic load of ~8.8 per 6-piece serving, making the standalone snack portion manageable for most dietary contexts. Sodium concern escalates with larger portions or dipping sauces. Clinically, the product is ultra-processed, high in sodium relative to snack foods, and contains pro-inflammatory frying by-products; it is suitable as an occasional food rather than a dietary staple. Key nutrients per 100g: 263 kcal, 14.3g protein, 14.3g fat (2.0g saturated), 16.3g carbohydrate, 439mg sodium, 0.8mg iron, 4.0mg niacin.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from McDonald's Singapore official nutrition data (exact brand-owner source for this specific commercial product). Per-100g values derived by linear scaling from the verified 98g serving. Micronutrient values cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central for plausibility. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence) based on University of Sydney GI Database comparable foods; overall record confidence is high for macros and moderate for GI estimate.
notes: Per-100g values derived by scaling from the verified 98g (6-piece) serving: energy 258 kcal ÷ 0.98 = 263.3 kcal/100g; protein 14g ÷ 0.98 = 14.3g; fat 14g ÷ 0.98 = 14.3g; carbohydrate 16g ÷ 0.98 = 16.3g; sodium 430mg ÷ 0.98 = 438.8mg. Sugar_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per McDonald's Singapore official nutritional data — the wheat batter coating contains no added sugars and the chicken meat contributes negligible sugar. Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: confirmed zero per McDonald's Singapore nutritional data; deep-fried chicken nuggets contain no meaningful vitamin C or vitamin D. Trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero per McDonald's Singapore nutritional data (palm oil frying medium, no partially hydrogenated oils used). Monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in McDonald's Singapore official nutritional data and not available in USDA Branded Foods for this specific Singapore-market product. Water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in McDonald's Singapore official nutritional data. GI ~55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for McDonald's Chicken McNuggets exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of April 2026. According to the University of Sydney GI Database, a GI of 55 is classified as 'low'. Dipping sauces are not included in these values; each sauce adds 25–50 kcal and 190–260mg sodium. McDonald's Singapore 2024 menu update removed artificial preservatives from the nugget coating. The 6-piece (98g) serving is the standard smallest size available at McDonald's Singapore; also available in 9-piece, 20-piece, and 40-piece.
